Package org.eventb.core.ast

Class Formula<T extends Formula<T>>

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.Formula<T>
Type Parameters:
T - TODO comment type parameter
Direct Known Subclasses:
Assignment, BoundIdentDecl, Expression, Predicate
public abstract class Formula<T extends Formula<T>> extends Object
Formula is the abstract base class for all nodes of an event-B formula AST (Abstract Syntax Tree).

To instantiate sub-classes of this class, use FormulaFactory.

Important Remark: All AST nodes are immutable. Once a node has been constructed, its contents can't be changed anymore. Except for its type. The type of an AST node must be changed by the type-checker.

Since:
1.0

    translate(FormulaFactory factory)

    translateDatatype(IDatatypeTranslation translation)

    Returns the type-checked formula obtained by translating this formula using the given extension translation.

    • NO_TAG

      public static final int NO_TAG
      NO_TAG is used as a placeholder when one needs to indicate that a tag value is invalid or absent. It is different from all valid tags.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • FREE_IDENT

      public static final int FREE_IDENT
      FREE_IDENT represents a free occurence of an identifer. Can only be a FreeIdentifier AST node.
      See Also:

      BOUND_IDENT_DECL represents a declaration of a bound identifer (within a quantified formula). Can only be a BoundIdentDecl AST node.

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Type type)
      Ensures that the formula factory of the given type is the same as the formula factory of this formula. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      type - type to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Formula<?>[] children)
      Ensures that the formula factory of each of the given formulas is the same as the formula factory of this formula. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      children - formulas to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the given formulas has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Formula<?> left, Formula<?> right)
      Ensures that the formula factory of each of the given formulas is the same as the formula factory of this formula. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      left - formula to check
      right - formula to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any of the given formulas has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(Formula<?> child)
      Ensures that the formula factory of the given formulas is the same as the formula factory of this formula. Throws an IllegalArgumentException otherwise.
      Parameters:
      child - formula to check
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given formulas has a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • ensureSameFactory

      protected void ensureSameFactory(ITypeEnvironment typEnv)
      Ensures that the formula factory of this formula and the factory of this type environment are the same. If it is not the case an IllegalArgumentException exception is raised.
      Since:
      3.0

    • getTag

      public final int getTag()
      Returns the tag of this AST node.

      Each node has an attached tag that represents the operator associated to it. This tag is immutable. The tags can be found in class Formula

      Returns:
      Returns the tag.

    • getSourceLocation

      public final SourceLocation getSourceLocation()
      Returns the source location of this AST node.

      Each node parsed from a string has an attached source location that describes where it is located in the original string. This location is immutable.

      If the formula was not created when parsing a string, the location is a null reference.

      Returns:
      Returns the source location or null.

    • toStringFullyParenthesized

      public final String toStringFullyParenthesized()
      Returns a string representation of the formula.

      The string contains as many parenthesis as possible.

      Returns:
      a string representation of this formula.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns the string representation of the formula.

      The string contains a minimal number of parenthesis, that is only parenthesis that are needed for the formula to be parsed again into the same AST.

      The string representation doesn't contain any type information.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      Returns the string representation of this formula.

    • toStringWithTypes

      public final String toStringWithTypes()
      Returns the string representation of the formula, including some type information.

      The string representation is the same as that returned by toString(), but with type information added to generic atomic expressions (empty sets and bound identifier declarations).

      Returns:
      Returns the string representation of this formula with type information.

    • toString

      protected abstract void toString(IToStringMediator mediator)
      Since:
      2.0

    • getKind

      protected abstract int getKind(KindMediator mediator)
      Since:
      2.0

    • getFactory

      public final FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Returns the formula factory used to build this formula.
      Returns:
      the formula factory used to build this formula
      Since:
      3.0

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object obj)
      Indicates whether some other formula is identical to this one.

      Comparison is done on the recursive structure of both formulas (deep equality). However, equality is considered modulo alpha-conversion (names of bound identifiers are ignored). Also, all forms of the same quantified expressions are considered equals. Finally, source locations are considered as pure decorations and are therefore not taken into account.

      For instance, the following formulas are considered equal (as they are alpha-equivalent): 

             ∀x,y·x ∈ ℕ ∧ y ∈ ℕ ⇒ x + y ∈ ℕ
       ∀z,t·z ∈ ℕ ∧ t ∈ ℕ ⇒ z + t ∈ ℕ

      Similarly, the following formulas are considered equal (as they are various forms of the same quantified expression); 

             λx·x ∈ ℕ | x + 1
       {x ↦ (x + 1) | x ∈ ℕ}
       {x·x ∈ ℕ | x ↦ (x + 1)}

      On the contrary, the following formulas are not equal (as x and y are interchanged in the consequent): 

             ∀x,y·x ∈ ℕ ∧ y ∈ ℕ ⇒ x + y ∈ ℕ
       ∀x,y·x ∈ ℕ ∧ y ∈ ℕ ⇒ y + x ∈ ℕ

      If either formula has been type-checked, then the comparison takes into account the types of formulas. Otherwise, the comparison is only done at a syntactical level.

      For instance, two copies of the formula x = ∅ are considered as equal when they are not typed. However, if one is typed (where x is a set of booleans) and not the other, they're not equal anymore. Similarly, if one has been type-checked with x being a set of booleans and the other with x being a set of integers, they are not equal.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the reference object with which to compare.
      Returns:
      true iff this formula is the same as the given argument
      See Also:
      • Object.equals(java.lang.Object)

    • flatten

      public final T flatten()
      Returns the flattened form of this formula.

      Flattening consists in performing the following operations on the formula:

      • Expanding inline the children of an associative expression which are of the same kind. For instance, "(x+x)+x" becomes "x+x+x".
      • Removing unused bound identifier declarations. For instance "∀x,y·x=a" becomes "∀x·x=a".
      • Regrouping quantifiers of quantified predicated of the same kind. For instance "∀x·∀y·x=y" becomes "∀x,y·x=y".
      • Replacing empty set extensions by empty sets: expression "{}" becomes "∅".
      • Replacing an integer literal preceded by an unary minus by an integer literal with the opposite value.

      If this formula was already flattened, then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula).

      Flattening is not supported for assignments.

      Returns:
      the formula in its flattened form
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment.
      Since:
      3.0

    • getFreeIdentifiers

      public final FreeIdentifier[] getFreeIdentifiers()
      Returns a list of all identifiers that occur free in this formula.

      This method uses directly the type-checker cache, so that it doesn't have to traverse the formula. Only one instance of each identifier occurring free in this formula is reported, hence all elements of the returned array are different.

      Since 3.0, all identifiers of given types occurring within this formula are also included in the result.

      Clients having special requirements on the order of identifiers should rather use getSyntacticallyFreeIdentifiers() to compute a sorted list of free identifiers of this formula (that latter method will indeed traverse the formula).

      Returns:
      an array of all free identifiers occurring in this formula
      See Also:
      • getSyntacticallyFreeIdentifiers()

    • getBoundIdentifiers

      public final BoundIdentifier[] getBoundIdentifiers()
      Returns a list of all identifiers that occur bound and are not declared within this formula.

      This method uses directly the type-checker cache, so that it doesn't have to traverse the formula.

      The identifiers returned are the identifiers with dangling de Bruijn indices. If the same identifier occurs more than once, only one occurrence is reported. Thus, all elements of the returned array are different.

      Returns:
      an array of all identifiers that occur bound and are not declared within this formula

    • getSyntacticallyFreeIdentifiers

      public final FreeIdentifier[] getSyntacticallyFreeIdentifiers()
      Returns a list of all identifiers that occur free in this formula.

      The actual elements of this list are the first (leftmost) free occurrence of each identifier. It is sorted in left-to-right order when reading the formula.

      If they have no requirements on the order of the free identifiers returned, clients should rather use getFreeIdentifiers() to compute the array of free identifiers.

      Returns:
      an array of all first free occurrences of identifiers.
      See Also:
      • getFreeIdentifiers()

    • getPredicateVariables

      public PredicateVariable[] getPredicateVariables()
      Returns a list of all predicate variables that occur within this formula.

      This method uses a cache, so that it doesn't have to traverse this formula each time it is called.

      Returns:
      an array of all predicate variables that occur within this formula
      Since:
      1.2

    • hasPredicateVariable

      public boolean hasPredicateVariable()
      Tells whether this formula contains predicate variables or not.
      Returns:
      true iff this formula contains some predicate variable
      Since:
      1.2

    • collectFreeIdentifiers

      protected abstract void collectFreeIdentifiers(LinkedHashSet<FreeIdentifier> freeIdentSet)
      Internal method.

      Collect the first (leftmost) free occurrence of all identifiers in this formula. These occurrences are stored by side-effect in set freeIdents.

      Parameters:
      freeIdentSet - freeIdentifiers collected so far

    • collectNamesAbove

      protected abstract void collectNamesAbove(Set<String> names, String[] boundNames, int offset)
      Internal method.

      Collect all names that occur in this formula and do not correspond to a locally bound identifier. These names are stored by side-effect in set names.

      For instance when called with boundIdents being "x", "y" and offset being 10 on the formula 

                { z . z : INTEGER | z + t + [[5]] } \/ [[11]]
      this method does the following:
      • "z" is not added to names, as it is locally bound by the comprehensive set.
      • "t" is added to names, as it occurs free.
      • The bound identifier with index 5 is ignored (its index is less than the offset, so it means it is bound below the root of the global formula and thus not relevant to us).
      • The bound identifier with index 11 corresponds to name "x" (once the offset subtracted from the index) and is thus added to names.

      Parameters:
      names - identifier names collected so far (extended by side-effect)
      boundNames - names that we consider bound globally
      offset - offset introduced by local quantifiers so far

    • getSyntaxTree

      public final String getSyntaxTree()
      Returns the left-aligned string representation of the syntax tree.

      This method is for debugging purpose. It returns a string that represents the AST of this formula.

      Returns:
      Returns the printable syntax tree.

    • getSyntaxTree

      protected abstract String getSyntaxTree(String[] boundNames, String tabs)
      Internal method that is used to contruct the printable syntax tree.

      Method getSyntaxTree() in fact calls this method with an empty Identifier array and an empty string as parameters.

      Parameters:
      boundNames - the array of the quantified identifier in the scope of the node
      tabs - the number of tabs that are displayed before the string for the current node
      Returns:
      Returns the string of the syntax tree.

    • isLegible

      public final IResult isLegible(Collection<FreeIdentifier> context)
      Checks whether a formula is legible.

      For a formula to be legible, a bound identifier must not appear free in the formula, and a bound identifier can be bound at exactly one place.

      Parameters:
      context - collection of identifiers occurring free in the context of this formula or null.
      Returns:
      the result of the legibility checker

    • isWellFormed

      public final boolean isWellFormed()
      Checks whether this formula is well-formed.

      A formula is called well-formed iff all bound identifiers occurring in it are declared within that same formula. In other words, the formula contains no dangling de Bruijn indices.

      Returns:
      true iff the formula is well-formed

    • typeCheck

      public final ITypeCheckResult typeCheck(ITypeEnvironment environment)
      Statically type-checks this well-formed formula. The type-check procedure traverses this formula and tries to infer the types of all sub-expressions. The type of some free identifiers can be specified by passing a non-empty type environment.

      Type-checking fails if some type could not be inferred or if some inconsistency is detected during the traversal (e.g., an illegal combination of child types or inconsistent typing of identifiers). In this case, all sub-formulas for which a type could be inferred are then type-checked, but not this formula.

      If this formula is already type-checked, the type-checking can nevertheless fail if the type of some free identifier of this formula is not compatible with the contents of the given type environment. However, the type-checked status of this formula will not be modified.

      Returns the ITypeCheckResult containing all information about the type-check run.

      Parameters:
      environment - an initial type environment
      Returns:
      the result of the type checker
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - is this formulas is not well-formed
      See Also:
      • isWellFormed()
      • isTypeChecked()
      • Expression.getType()
      • BoundIdentDecl.getType()

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • bindAllFreeIdents

      public final T bindAllFreeIdents(List<BoundIdentDecl> boundIdentDecls)
      Returns a copy of this formula where all identifiers that occur free in it become bound at the root. The list of these identifiers is stored in boundIdent as a side effect. It then can be used to create a quantified Formula.

      For instance, when given as input the predicate: 

            ∀x·x = y ∧ y = z ∧ y = { z | z ∈ y }
      the list of identifiers to bind is 
            y, z
      and the result formula is (De Bruijn numbers are shown in square bracket after each bound identifier): 
            !x.x[0] = y[2] ∧ y[2] = z[1] ∧ y[2] = { z[0] | z[0] ∈ y[3] }

      The order in the list boundIdent is determined by the first occurence of each identifier, when reading the formula from left to right.

      This operation is not supported by assignments.

      Parameters:
      boundIdentDecls - initially an empty list. Upon return, the list is filled with the declaration of the bound identifiers that correspond to all free identifiers that have been bound in the resut.
      Returns:
      a copy of this formula where all free identifiers became bound.
      Since:
      3.0

    • bindTheseIdents

      public final T bindTheseIdents(Collection<FreeIdentifier> identsToBind)
      Returns a copy of this formula where all occurrences of identifiers in identsToBind have been bound.

      The collection of identifiers passed as parameter is ordered. The order is defined by the iterator associated to the collection. The exact same order must be used to create the quantified formula that will contain the result.

      If the result formula would be the same as this formula (none of the given identifiers occurred free in this formula), a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula).

      This operation is not supported by assignments.

      Parameters:
      identsToBind - ordered collection of free identifier to bind
      Returns:
      a copy of this formula with the specified identifiers bound
      Since:
      3.0

    • getTypedThis

      protected abstract T getTypedThis()

    • applyAssignment

      public final T applyAssignment(BecomesEqualTo assignment)
      Applies the given assignment to this formula.

      This method builds a new formula where each occurrence of a free identifier which is assigned to, is replaced by the corresponding expression in the assignment. All substitutions are done in parallel.

      For instance, applying the assignment x := 0 to the formula x + y gives the result formula 0 + y. Similarly, applying x := x + 1 to x + y gives (x + 1) + y. Finally, applying x,y := y,x to x + y gives y + x.

      This operation is not supported by assignments.

      Parameters:
      assignment - the assignment to apply
      Returns:
      this formula with the given assignment applied to it
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • applyAssignments(Iterable)

    • applyAssignments

      public final T applyAssignments(Iterable<BecomesEqualTo> assignments)
      Applies in parallel the given assignments to this formula.

      This method builds a new formula where each occurrence of a free identifier which is assigned to, is replaced by the corresponding expression in the assignment. All substitutions are done in parallel.

      The assignments given as input must not assign twice the same identifier: their left-hand sides must be pairwise disjoint.

      This operation is not supported by assignments.

      Parameters:
      assignments - the assignments to apply
      Returns:
      this formula with the given assignments applied to it
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • applyAssignment(BecomesEqualTo)

    • addAssignmentToMap

      private static void addAssignmentToMap(Map<FreeIdentifier,Expression> map, BecomesEqualTo assignment)

    • rewrite

      public T rewrite(IFormulaRewriter rewriter)
      Rewrites this formula using the given rewriter. The rewriting operation is performed in a depth-first, post-order traversal. This means that the formula tree is traversed depth-first and the rewriter is called for each node of the formula. The calls to the rewriter are done in post-order: children are rewritten before their parent.

      Additionaly, each time a quantified formula is traversed, method IFormulaRewriter.enteringQuantifier(int) (resp. IFormulaRewriter.leavingQuantifier(int)) is called just before (resp. after) processing the children of the quantified formula.

      If no rewrite was performed on this formula (that is all rewrite calls on sub-formulas returned an identical sub-formula), then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula). This allows to test efficiently (using ==) whether rewriting made any change.

      This operation is not supported for assignments and bound identifier declarations. The returned formula is type-checked if this formula is type-checked.

      Parameters:
      rewriter - the rewriter to apply
      Returns:
      this formula with the given rewriter applied to it
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment or a bound identifier declaration.
      IllegalArgumentException - if, at any point, the sub-formula returned by the rewriter is incompatible with the original sub-formula:
      • the original sub-formula was type-checked and the new sub-formula is not type-checked.
      • the new subformula bears a different type from the original sub-formula.
      See Also:
      • IFormulaRewriter

    • rewrite

      protected abstract T rewrite(ITypeCheckingRewriter rewriter)
      This method is not part part of the published API of the AST library and shall not be called by clients. However, interface ITypeCheckingRewriter is not part of the API on purpose.
      Parameters:
      rewriter - some type-checking rewriter
      Returns:
      the rewritten formula

    • substituteFreeIdents

      public T substituteFreeIdents(Map<FreeIdentifier,Expression> map)
      Substitutes all occurrences of some free identifiers by their corresponding expressions as specified by the given map.

      For each entry of the given map, the free identifier and the replacement expression must both be typed and bear the same type.

      If no change where performed on this formula (none of the identifier occurred free in this formula), then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula).

      This operation is not supported by assignments.

      Parameters:
      map - The substitution to be carried out
      Returns:
      this formula after application of the substitution
      Since:
      3.0

    • equalsInternal

      protected abstract boolean equalsInternal(Formula<?> other)
      Tells whether two formulae are equal modulo alpha-conversion. In other terms, whether the two formulae have the same tree, where the names of bound identifier declarations are ignored. Tags are already known to be the same. This method must only be called by equals(Object), and never directly by recursive calls.
      Parameters:
      other - the formula to be compared to
      Returns:
      true if both objects are equal
      Since:
      3.0

    • typeCheck

      protected abstract void typeCheck(TypeCheckResult result, BoundIdentDecl[] quantifiedIdentifiers)
      Internal methods that statically type-checks the formula.
      Parameters:
      result - the result which is modified throughout the formula.
      quantifiedIdentifiers - a list of bound identifier declarations above this formula.

    • isLegible

      protected abstract void isLegible(LegibilityResult result)
      Internal method that checks whether a formula is well-formed.

      For a formula to be well-formed, a bound identifier must not appear free in the formula, and a bound identifier can be bound at exactly one place.

      Method isLegible(Collection) in fact calls this method with a new result and an empty array.

      Parameters:
      result - the LegibilityResult that is used to return information about the errors found. Can not be null
      Since:
      2.0

    • getWDPredicate

      public final Predicate getWDPredicate()
      Computes the Well-Definedness predicate for this formula.

      The following optimizations are implemented:

      • "⊤" is eliminated from formulas where possible
      • duplicate occurrences of the same predicate are eliminated
      • implications are replaced by conjunctions
      according to the equivalences 
                      (⊤ ∧ A) ⇔ A
                (A ∧ ⊤) ⇔ A
                (A ∧ A) ⇔ A
                (A ∨ ⊤) ⇔ ⊤
                (⊤ ∨ A) ⇔ ⊤
                (A ⇒ (B ⇒ C)) ⇔ (A ∧ B ⇒ C)
                (A ⇒ ⊤) ⇔ ⊤
                (⊤ ⇒ A) ⇔ A
                (A ⇒ A) ⇔ ⊤
                (∀x·⊤) ⇔ ⊤
                (∃x·⊤) ⇔ ⊤
                (∀x·A) ⇔ A provided x nfin A
                (∃x·A) ⇔ A provided x nfin A

      This formula must be type-checked before getWDPredicate() can be invoked.

      Returns:
      the well-definedness predicate for this formula.
      Since:
      3.0

    • isWDStrict

      public abstract boolean isWDStrict()
      Returns whether this formula node is WD strict. This node is WD strict if its well-definedness implies the well-definedness of all of its children, for all WD syntactic transformations (D or L). For instance, addition is WD strict, while conjunction is not.
      Returns:
      true iff this node is WD strict
      Since:
      2.0

    • solveType

      protected abstract void solveType(TypeUnifier unifier)
      Internal method used by the type-checker to set the type of the formula after a type-check has been executed.
      Parameters:
      unifier - a type unifier
      Since:
      3.0

    • accept

      public abstract boolean accept(IVisitor visitor)
      Traverses this formula with the given visitor. In this complex version, the accept method also manages stepping through AST children nodes.
      Parameters:
      visitor - the visitor to call back during traversal
      Returns:
      true to continue traversal, false to take some shortcut.
      See Also:
      • IVisitor

    • accept

      public abstract void accept(ISimpleVisitor visitor)
      Accept the visit of this formula with the given simple visitor. In this simple version, this method only calls the visit method of the visitor corresponding to the dynamic class of the formula. Ast traversal is not managed by this method and shall be implemented in the visit methods of the client.
      Parameters:
      visitor - the visitor to call back during traversal
      See Also:
      • ISimpleVisitor

    • isTypeChecked

      public final boolean isTypeChecked()
      Returns whether this formula has been type-checked. A formula can be type-checked by construction (e.g., an integer literal) or by running the type checker. If this formula is marked as type-checked, then it is also the case of all its descendants. Once a formula has been type-checked, this method will always return true.
      Returns:
      true iff this formula has been type-checked
      See Also:
      • typeCheck(ITypeEnvironment)
      • Expression.typeCheck(ITypeEnvironment, Type)

    • shiftBoundIdentifiers

      public T shiftBoundIdentifiers(int offset)
      Returns a copy of this formula where all externally bound identifier indexes have been shifted by the given offset.

      If no change where performed on this formula, then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of this formula).

      Parameters:
      offset - offset to apply to bound identifier indexes. Use the number of bound identifier declarations that you plan to add just atop this formula. If you plan to remove some bound identifier declarations, then use a negative offset
      Returns:
      a copy of this formula with all bound identifiers shifted by the given offset
      Since:
      3.0

    • getGivenTypes

      public final Set<GivenType> getGivenTypes()
      Returns a set of all given types which are used for typing this formula. This method uses the free identifier cache of the formula to extract the given types used.
      Returns:
      a set containing all given types which are used in this formula types

    • getSubFormula

      public final Formula<?> getSubFormula(IPosition position)
      Returns the sub-formula at the given position in this formula, or null if the given position does not correspond to any sub-formula of this formula.
      Parameters:
      position - the position of the sub-formula to retrieve
      Returns:
      the sub-formula at the given position in this formula, or null if there is none

    • isWDStrict

      public boolean isWDStrict(IPosition position)
      Returns whether the given position is WD strict in this formula. A position is WD strict if the well-definedness of this formula implies the well-definedness of the subformula occurring at the given position. If the given position does not correspond to any sub-formula of this formula, then this method returns false.
      Parameters:
      position - position to consider in this formula
      Returns:
      true iff the given position is WD strict in this formula
      Since:
      2.0

    • getChild

      public abstract Formula<?> getChild(int index)
      Returns the child at the given index. Child indexes are counted from 0 for the first child to getChildCount() - 1. for the last one.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Parameters:
      index - index of the child to retrieve
      Returns:
      the child at the given index
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - is the index is not in the appropriate range
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      Since:
      2.1

    • getChildCount

      public abstract int getChildCount()
      Returns the number of children of this formula.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Returns:
      the number of children
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      Since:
      2.1

    • checkChildIndex

      protected final void checkChildIndex(int index)
      Since:
      2.1

    • invalidIndex

      protected static IllegalArgumentException invalidIndex(int index)
      Since:
      2.1

    • getPositions

      public final List<IPosition> getPositions(IFormulaFilter filter)
      Returns the positions of all sub-formulas of this formula that satisfy the given criterion.

      The positions are computed by calling the filter on each node of the formula tree, traversed in pre-order. Consequently, the returned list is always sorted lexicographically.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Parameters:
      filter - filter implementing the criterion to test for
      Returns:
      a list of the positions of all sub-formulas that satisfy the given criterion

    • inspect

      public final <F> List<F> inspect(IFormulaInspector<F> inspector)
      Returns the findings for all sub-formulas of this formula that are reported by the given inspector.

      The findings are obtained by calling the inspector on each node of the formula tree, traversed in pre-order. The inspector can also skip some sub-formulas.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Type Parameters:
      F - type of the findings to report
      Parameters:
      inspector - the inspector used for analyzing sub-formulas
      Returns:
      a list of all the findings reported by the inspector during traversal
      Since:
      2.0

    • inspect

      protected abstract <F> void inspect(FindingAccumulator<F> acc)
      Since:
      2.0

    • getPosition

      public final IPosition getPosition(SourceLocation sloc)
      Returns the position of the deepest sub-formula of this formula that contains the given source location.

      In case there are several sub-formulas that contain the given source location, and if they are not in a ancester-descendant relationship (that case being tackled with by picking up the descendant), the position returned is that of the sub-formulas which is encountered first in a top-down traversal of this formula.

      This method is not applicable to assignments.

      Parameters:
      sloc - source location to search
      Returns:
      the position of the deepest sub-formula that contains the given source location, or null if there is none

    • getPosition

      protected final IPosition getPosition(SourceLocation sloc, IntStack indexes)

    • getDescendantPos

      protected abstract IPosition getDescendantPos(SourceLocation sloc, IntStack indexes)

    • contains

      public final boolean contains(SourceLocation sloc)
      Tells whether this formula spans the given source location. In other words, returns true if the source location associated to this formula is not null and contains the given source location, false otherwise.
      Parameters:
      sloc - another source location
      Returns:
      true iff this formula spans the given location
      See Also:
      • SourceLocation.contains(SourceLocation)

    • rewriteSubFormula

      public final T rewriteSubFormula(IPosition position, Formula<?> newFormula)
      Returns a new formula obtained from this formula by replacing the sub-formula at the given position by the given new formula.

      The given position must designate a sub-formula. The replaced and new sub-formula must be of the same kind (bound identifier declaration, expression or predicate), and be both type-checked. Moreover, they must bear the same type (except for predicates which do not bear a type).

      This operation is not supported for assignments, nor untyped formulas. The returned formula is type-checked.

      Parameters:
      position - the position of the sub-formula to rewrite
      newFormula - the new sub-formula to replace with
      Returns:
      a copy of this formula where the sub-formula at the given position has been replaced by the given new sub-formula
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment.
      IllegalStateException - if this formula is not type-checked.
      IllegalArgumentException - in the following cases:
      • the position does not lie inside this formula,
      • the replaced and new sub-formulas are incompatible (different kind or different type).
      Since:
      3.0

    • rewriteChild

      protected abstract T rewriteChild(int index, SingleRewriter rewriter)

    • getCheckedReplacement

      protected abstract T getCheckedReplacement(SingleRewriter rewriter)

    • ensureTypeChecked

      protected final void ensureTypeChecked()

    • specialize

      public T specialize(ISpecialization specialization)
      Returns the type-checked formula obtained by applying the given specialization to this formula.

      If the specialization does not change the formula, then a reference to this formula is returned (rather than a copy of it). This allows to test efficiently (using ==) whether specialization made any change.

      This operation is not supported for assignments. This formula must be type-checked.

      Parameters:
      specialization - the specialization to apply
      Returns:
      the formula obtained by applying the given specialization to this formula
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      IllegalStateException - if this formula is not type-checked.
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given specialization is not compatible with this formula, that is the specialization contains a substitution for an identifier with the same name as an identifier in this formula, but with a different type
      Since:
      2.6

    • translateDatatype

      public T translateDatatype(IDatatypeTranslation translation)
      Returns the type-checked formula obtained after translating out all the datatypes occurring within this formula using the given datatype translation.
      Parameters:
      translation - some datatype translation instance
      Returns:
      a type-checked formula where datatypes have been translated out
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      IllegalStateException - if this formula is not type-checked
      IllegalArgumentException - if this formula was built by another factory than the source factory of the given translation
      IllegalArgumentException - if this formula contains identifiers that are not in the source type environment of the given translation
      Since:
      2.7
      See Also:
      • ITypeEnvironment.makeDatatypeTranslation()

    • translateExtensions

      public T translateExtensions(IExtensionTranslation translation)
      Returns the type-checked formula obtained by translating this formula using the given extension translation.
      Parameters:
      translation - some extension translation
      Returns:
      a type-checked formula where most extensions have been translated to function applications
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      IllegalStateException - if this formula is not type-checked
      IllegalArgumentException - if this formula was built by a factory different from that of the source type environment of the given translation
      Since:
      3.1
      See Also:
      • ITypeEnvironment.makeExtensionTranslation()

    • isTranslatable

      public boolean isTranslatable(FormulaFactory factory)
      Checks if the current formula can be translated to the given factory.

      A formula can be translated to the given factory iff

      • no free identifier occurring in this formula becomes a reserved keyword,
      • all extensions occurring in this formula are supported,
      • all types occurring in this formula can be translated.

      This operation is not supported for assignments.

      Parameters:
      factory - the target formula factory
      Returns:
      true iff a call to translate(FormulaFactory) would succeed
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      Since:
      3.0

    • translate

      public T translate(FormulaFactory factory)
      Returns a copy of this formula built with the given formula factory.

      If the given factory is the formula factory that built this formula, then this formula is returned, rather than a copy of it.

      The translation of this formula will fail if the preconditions tested by isTranslatable(FormulaFactory) are not fulfilled.

      This operation is not supported for assignments.

      Parameters:
      factory - the target formula factory
      Returns:
      a copy of this formula built with the given formula factory
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if preconditions are not fulfilled
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this formula is an assignment
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • isTranslatable(FormulaFactory)
